title: woodsia pusilla var. cathcartiana
intro: Woodsia pusilla var. cathcartiana, also known as the Cathcart's Woodsia or Cathcart's Fern, is a rare and endangered species of fern belonging to the family Woodsiaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Woodsia pusilla var. cathcartiana include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antioxidant activity. In herbal preparations, it is often used in the form of infusions or decoctions, which can be consumed as teas or used as topical applications for skin conditions and wounds.
